entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The crop species being studied, specifically the Valencia type germplasm

entities.name: CORE COLLECTION
entities.description: A representative subset of a larger germplasm collection used to preserve genetic diversity

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL AND AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Descriptors used to evaluate and cluster peanut accessions

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Applied programs for cultivar development and genetic improvement

entities.name: AUGMENTED DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used for evaluating descriptors over two seasons

entities.name: WARD'S METHOD
entities.description: The clustering method used to group accessions based on descriptors

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat markers used for molecular characterization

entities.name: UNITED STATES
entities.description: The country of origin for the USDA peanut germplasm

entities.name: CO-ADAPTED GENE COMPLEXES
entities.description: Genetic associations preserved within the core subset

relationships.description: A core collection was developed for Valencia peanut germplasm
The core collection was selected based on morphological and agronomic descriptors
The core collection serves as a gateway for applied breeding programs
Augmented design was used to evaluate the descriptors
Ward's method was used for clustering to develop the core subset
SSR markers were used for molecular characterization of the peanut collection
The peanut germplasm was sourced from the USDA in the United States
The core subset preserved most of the co-adapted gene complexes
